raw_result.description: SEO platform with a REST API for pulling Ahrefs workspace data into custom integrations and automations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official API docs specify authentication via an API key sent as a Bearer token in the HTTP Authorization header (`Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY`); keys are created and managed in Ahrefs account settings and expire after 1 year.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official docs say the API is available on eligible paid plans and the MCP docs require a paid plan (Lite or higher), while other plans only get limited free test queries for development/testing.

api_surface.breadth: Ahrefs API v3 exposes workspace data and operations for products such as Site Explorer, Keywords Explorer, Site Audit, Rank Tracker, and related custom integrations/automations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Ahrefs officially documents and hosts an MCP server for AI agents at `https://api.ahrefs.com/mcp/mcp`. The MCP docs say it requires a paid plan (Lite or higher).

raw_result.blocker: A first toolkit is buildable from public docs, but production use requires a paid Ahrefs plan/API entitlement; only limited free test queries are available without that paid access.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official docs state a default limit of 60 requests per minute; exceeding the limit returns HTTP 429, and throttling may also occur under high load.
raw_result.notable_signals: Ahrefs provides both official API v3 docs and official MCP docs, plus API-unit based usage accounting and free test queries for development.

result.description: SEO platform with a REST API for pulling Ahrefs workspace data into custom integrations and automations.
result.auth_notes: Official API docs specify authentication via an API key sent as a Bearer token in the HTTP Authorization header (`Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY`); keys are created and managed in Ahrefs account settings and expire after 1 year.
result.gating_reason: Official docs say the API is available on eligible paid plans and the MCP docs require a paid plan (Lite or higher), while other plans only get limited free test queries for development/testing.

api_surface.mcp_notes: Ahrefs officially documents and hosts an MCP server for AI agents at `https://api.ahrefs.com/mcp/mcp`. The MCP docs say it requires a paid plan (Lite or higher).

result.rate_limit_notes: Official docs state a default limit of 60 requests per minute; exceeding the limit returns HTTP 429, and throttling may also occur under high load.
